mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.1% marks an extreme accommodative posture. Sustained low rates compress discount rates, raising present values of future equity cash flows and driving forward returns higher over a one-month horizon. This mechanism operates continuously regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns remains above -0.10 (i.e., no positive association) over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 8.4% exceeds the 7% threshold that historically marks severe labor market distress. Such conditions depress household income and consumption, lowering corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading month.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE > 7%, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a synchronized weakening of consumer and housing demand. This joint contraction reduces aggregate demand and corporate profitability, driving negative equity returns over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline and HOUST month-over-month decline
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, the average 60-day forward return is non-negative or the proportion of negative returns fails to exceed 55% over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In the current environment of elevated consumer prices (CPIAUCSL > 260), an increase in industrial production (INDPRO month-over-month > 0.5%) signals potential overheating. Capacity constraints and rising input costs outweigh output gains, compressing margins and leading to negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In months satisfying the trigger, 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.25%, from the current extreme low of 0.1%, represents the initiation of monetary tightening. Historically, such liftoffs compress valuations by raising discount rates and signal reduced policy support, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In the 20-day window following all FEDFUNDS > 0.25% events, the average return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or negative returns occur in fewer than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) measures the overall economic output; a positive trend supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. Despite the quarterly lag, sustained GDP growth drives positive longer-term forward returns as expansions feed into profit expectations.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day forward returns is negative or insignificantly positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The concurrence of a high unemployment level (UNRATE > 7%) and a monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS decrease) indicates accelerating labor market deterioration. This joint signal amplifies fears of recession, depressing consumer spending and equity prices over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO > 1% month-over-month) signals robust manufacturing activity and broad economic momentum. This positive supply-side impulse boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent trading month.
failure_condition: In months where INDPRO grows >1%, the 20-day forward return is negative on average, or the proportion of positive returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10) over any 5-year evaluation window.
